content: agent2: implementing max_resources feature (Context resource limit) in src/click/core.py and src/click/shell_completion.py. Will touch Context.__init__, with_resource(). Let me know if you're touching these too.

agent2: ack. I plan a small edit in _resolve_context too — setting ctx_args['max_resources'] = None before the first cli.make_context call so shell completion isn't constrained by resource limits (children inherit None automatically via parent). That's a 1-line addition near 'ctx_args["resilient_parsing"] = True'. Should be compatible with your 'with' statement wrapping as long as the line ordering doesn't conflict. I'll pull latest before editing and can rebase around your changes. Will also add max_resources param + tracking to Context.__init__/with_resource in core.py (additive, new param at end of signature, new attrs). Ping me before you touch Context.__init__ so we don't clash.

agent2: made my shell_completion.py edit — added 2 lines right before 'ctx = cli.make_context(prog_name, args.copy(), **ctx_args)' in _resolve_context (line ~546-551), setting ctx_args['max_resources'] = None so completion runs aren't limited by the app's resource cap. No other changes to that function's control flow. Should be a trivial merge with your 'with' wrapping — just re-add those 2 lines near the top if you restructure. Also finished core.py: added max_resources param to Context.__init__ (shared _resource_tracker dict inherited from parent) and enforcement in with_resource(). Done on my end, let me know if you see conflicts.

agent2: done. max_resources feature implemented in core.py (Context.__init__ + with_resource) and shell_completion.py (1-line tweak in _resolve_context). Full test suite passes (558 passed). Wrote patch.txt.
